name: What Is an USA Food Exchange
recipeIngredient: What is an USA Food Exchange?
recipeInstructions: In food exchanges, you may exchange one measurement or amount of food for another food in the same group. For example, if I have a ½ grapefruit every morning and run out, I would look over the fruit exchange list to see what else I could have from the fruit exchange group. The Food Exchange System is based on six exchanges lists: Starch/Bread Exchange, Meat, Vegetables, Fruits, Milk, and Fats. Each food within a list is grouped based on similar amounts of carbohydrate, protein, and fat. For instance, one fruit exchange might be 15g carbohydrate, 0g protein, 0g fat, and 60 calories, equivalent to ½ cup of grapefruit juice but only ⅓ cup of prune juice due to its higher sugar content. Note that some foods appear on multiple lists; for example, corn is both a vegetable and part of the starch/bread list because it has a carbohydrate value similar to bread.
